vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

verify.value: All required fields are present and valid.

verify.rule: Each physical feature must map back to the design requirements.

risk.desc: Potential thermal management issues in the avionics bay due to insufficient cooling.
risk.mit: Implement additional heat sinks or active cooling systems.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if the fuselage spine cannot handle the combined loads from nacelles and wings.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and reinforce the spine if necessary.

risk.desc: Possibility of inadequate electrical connectivity leading to system failures.
risk.mit: Review and test all electrical connections thoroughly.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment during assembly due to complex interface geometries.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and perform dry runs.

risk.desc: Potential for service access limitations hindering maintenance operations.
risk.mit: Increase the size of service access points or add more access locations.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads transferred into fuselage spine via spar_socket
Heat dissipation from avionics bay into fuselage shell via heat sinks
Data and power lines run through harness_passthroughs to external antennas

integration_opportunities.description: Avionics bay provides space for additional sensors or communication modules
Service access cut allows easy replacement of onboard electronics
External surfaces can be customized with decals or paint schemes

verification_focus.description: Ensure the fuselage spine can withstand expected loads from nacelles and wings
Verify the avionics bay has adequate cooling to prevent overheating
Check all data and power lines are properly routed and connected
